title: Upgrade, CONNECT, Tunnels, WebSocket Boundaries, and Safe Transitions · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A privacy-sensitive partner gateway team. They need ordered persistent HTTP/1.1 connections, bounded expectations, trusted intermediary behavior, validated authority, TLS 1.3 identity and ALPN, and confirmed Upgrade and CONNECT handoff without optimistic bytes. Planned artifact: Deliver Persistent TLS and Protocol-Transition Gateway with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Close and discard connection state after ambiguous framing, parse failure, incomplete output, unexpected residual bytes, or state disagreement.
Test client and server state transitions under delayed, missing, duplicate, fragmented, and rejected expectations without deadlock.
Assign parse, policy, connect, timeout, upstream, transform, and downstream-write failures to the correct hop and safe response boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Desynchronization Close
steps.instruction:
project case — A project protocol team handles case 9838 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to wait for confirmed Upgrade or CONNECT acceptance before transferring parser ownership; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Project Desynchronization Close.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for close and discard connection state after ambiguous framing, parse failure, incomplete output, unexpected residual bytes, or state disagreement. For Commit to the mission model · Project Desynchronization Close, keep Desynchronization Close connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
